title: San Juan County, Washington - Private Property

sections.title: Emergency
sections.content:
**Property Address:** 667 Bear Cove Rd, Deer Harbor, WA 98243

**Status:** Documented

### Generator Instructions
![Generator Instructions](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/FdqOlUIjWfQvyDzB.jpg)

**Steps to switch to generator power:**
1. Start generator (Located behind Camp Kitchen Shelter)
2. Turn off House Main Switch #1
3. Turn off main Switch on panel #2
4. Switch #3 Transfer Switch to Gen. set
5. Turn on House Main Switch #1
6. Turn on Main Switch on panel #2

**To switch back to OPALCO power:**
1. Turn off House Main Switch #1
2. Turn off main Switch on panel #2
3. Switch #3 Transfer Switch to OPALCO
4. Turn off generator
5. Turn on House Main Switch #1
6. Turn on Main Switch on panel #2

### Critical Contacts
- **Emergency:** 911
- **Caretaker (Joseph):** 360-982-9705
- **Caretaker (Lilliana):** 360-317-6121
- **Power (OPALCO):** 360-376-3500

sections.title: Workflow Guide
sections.content:
# How to Add Photos to the Property Manual (Android)

**Simple Step-by-Step Guide for Adding Photos from Your Phone**

---

## What You Need

- Your Android phone with photos
- The property manual website link
- Internet connection

---

## The Process (Super Simple Version)

### Step 1: Take Your Photos

Walk around the property and take photos of whatever you want to document (water heaters, electrical panels, valves, etc.). Don't worry about organizing them yet—just take the pictures.

### Step 2: Open the Property Manual Website

On your phone or computer, open this link:

**https://3000-ij5bpzk4aks2mnrac8550-bd361b2c.us2.manus.computer/photo-labeling**

This is the photo upload page.

### Step 3: Upload Your Photos

**On your phone:**
- Tap the gray upload box
- Select "Choose Files" or "Browse"
- Pick all the photos you want to upload (you can select multiple at once)
- Wait for them to load (you'll see them appear numbered: Photo #1, Photo #2, etc.)

**On your computer:**
- Drag and drop the photos into the gray box, OR
- Click "Select Files" and choose them from your folders

### Step 4: Label Each Photo

For each photo, type a label in the text box below it. Use this format:

**[Building/System] - [What It Is] - [Where It Is]**

**Examples:**
- `Main House - Water Heater - Utility Room`
- `Farm House - Electrical Panel - Garage`
- `Irrigation System - Valve Box A - Front Yard`
- `Well System - Pressure Tank - Pump House`

**Tips:**
- If you don't remember what a photo is, just skip it (leave the box empty)
- Photos without labels will be deleted automatically
- Your progress saves automatically—you can close the page and come back later

### Step 5: Export Your Labels

When you're done labeling (or need to take a break):
1. Click the **"Export Labels"** button at the top
2. A text file will download to your phone/computer
3. Send that file to me (Manus) in the chat

### Step 6: I Take Over

Once you send me the labels file:
- I'll upload all the photos to the cloud (CDN)
- I'll organize them into the right sections of the manual
- I'll update the website so you can see them
- I'll save a checkpoint

**That's it!** You take the photos, label them, and I handle the rest.

---

## Quick Reference

| Step | What You Do | Where |
|------|-------------|-------|
| 1 | Take photos with your phone | Out in the field |
| 2 | Open photo upload page | https://3000-ij5bpzk4aks2mnrac8550-bd361b2c.us2.manus.computer/photo-labeling |
| 3 | Upload photos | Tap/click upload box |
| 4 | Label each photo | Type: Building - Item - Location |
| 5 | Export labels | Click "Export Labels" button |
| 6 | Send me the file | In Manus chat |

---

## Common Questions

**Q: Can I upload photos from my phone's camera roll?**  
A: Yes! You can upload any photos from your phone, whether you just took them or they're from weeks ago.

**Q: How many photos can I upload at once?**  
A: As many as you want! The page can handle hundreds of photos.

**Q: What if I forget to label a photo?**  
A: No problem—just leave it blank. I'll automatically skip any photos without labels.

**Q: Can I edit labels after I export them?**  
A: Yes! Just open the text file and edit the labels before sending it to me.

**Q: What if I close the page before I'm done?**  
A: Your progress saves automatically. Just reopen the same link and your labels will still be there.

**Q: Do I need to organize photos into folders first?**  
A: Nope! Just upload them all at once and label them. I'll organize them into the right sections of the manual.

---

## Need Help?

If you get stuck, just send me a message in the chat and I'll walk you through it!

subsections.title: Photo Upload Procedure
subsections.content:
# How to Add Photos to the Property Manual (Android)

**Simple Step-by-Step Guide for Adding Photos from Your Phone**

---

## What You Need

- Your Android phone with photos
- The property manual website link
- Internet connection

---

## The Process (Super Simple Version)

### Step 1: Take Your Photos

Walk around the property and take photos of whatever you want to document (water heaters, electrical panels, valves, etc.). Don't worry about organizing them yet—just take the pictures.

### Step 2: Open the Property Manual Website

On your phone or computer, open this link:

**https://3000-ij5bpzk4aks2mnrac8550-bd361b2c.us2.manus.computer/photo-labeling**

This is the photo upload page.

### Step 3: Upload Your Photos

**On your phone:**
- Tap the gray upload box
- Select "Choose Files" or "Browse"
- Pick all the photos you want to upload (you can select multiple at once)
- Wait for them to load (you'll see them appear numbered: Photo #1, Photo #2, etc.)

**On your computer:**
- Drag and drop the photos into the gray box, OR
- Click "Select Files" and choose them from your folders

### Step 4: Label Each Photo

For each photo, type a label in the text box below it. Use this format:

**[Building/System] - [What It Is] - [Where It Is]**

**Examples:**
- `Main House - Water Heater - Utility Room`
- `Farm House - Electrical Panel - Garage`
- `Irrigation System - Valve Box A - Front Yard`
- `Well System - Pressure Tank - Pump House`

**Tips:**
- If you don't remember what a photo is, just skip it (leave the box empty)
- Photos without labels will be deleted automatically
- Your progress saves automatically—you can close the page and come back later

### Step 5: Export Your Labels

When you're done labeling (or need to take a break):
1. Click the **"Export Labels"** button at the top
2. A text file will download to your phone/computer
3. Send that file to me (Manus) in the chat

### Step 6: I Take Over

Once you send me the labels file:
- I'll upload all the photos to the cloud (CDN)
- I'll organize them into the right sections of the manual
- I'll update the website so you can see them
- I'll save a checkpoint

**That's it!** You take the photos, label them, and I handle the rest.

---

## Quick Reference

| Step | What You Do | Where |
|------|-------------|-------|
| 1 | Take photos with your phone | Out in the field |
| 2 | Open photo upload page | https://3000-ij5bpzk4aks2mnrac8550-bd361b2c.us2.manus.computer/photo-labeling |
| 3 | Upload photos | Tap/click upload box |
| 4 | Label each photo | Type: Building - Item - Location |
| 5 | Export labels | Click "Export Labels" button |
| 6 | Send me the file | In Manus chat |

---

## Common Questions

**Q: Can I upload photos from my phone's camera roll?**  
A: Yes! You can upload any photos from your phone, whether you just took them or they're from weeks ago.

**Q: How many photos can I upload at once?**  
A: As many as you want! The page can handle hundreds of photos.

**Q: What if I forget to label a photo?**  
A: No problem—just leave it blank. I'll automatically skip any photos without labels.

**Q: Can I edit labels after I export them?**  
A: Yes! Just open the text file and edit the labels before sending it to me.

**Q: What if I close the page before I'm done?**  
A: Your progress saves automatically. Just reopen the same link and your labels will still be there.

**Q: Do I need to organize photos into folders first?**  
A: Nope! Just upload them all at once and label them. I'll organize them into the right sections of the manual.

---

## Need Help?

If you get stuck, just send me a message in the chat and I'll walk you through it!
subsections.intro: Step-by-step instructions for uploading and labeling photos from your Android phone to the property manual.

sections.intro:
**Purpose:** Instructions and procedures for maintaining and updating the property manual.

This section contains step-by-step guides for common tasks like uploading photos, adding content, and requesting updates. These procedures ensure consistent documentation and make it easy to keep the manual current.

sections.title: Farm House
sections.content:
**Parcel:** 260833006000 (23.74 acres - Shady Cove LLC) - [View on Polaris GIS](https://gis.sanjuancountywa.gov/polaris/)

### Structure & Exterior
- **Type:** Wood-frame farmhouse with traditional root cellar (built into hillside)
- **Exterior:** Wood siding with distinctive green/teal trim
- **Roof:** Gray asphalt shingles with dormers
- **Photo:** [Pending Upload]

### Kitchen Appliances
- **Refrigerator:** LG LRTP0931W (8.6 cu. ft. compact top-freezer) - *Rec. Replace: 2028*
- **Range:** Premier Electric (4-burner coil cooktop)
- **Amenities:** No dishwasher, microwave, or laundry facilities

### Electrical System
- **Panel:** MTS electrical panel in dining room with generator transfer capability
- **Circuits:** Complete handwritten directory available
- **Photo:** [Pending Upload]

### Heating System
- **Type:** Electric baseboard heating (multiple zones)

### Septic System
- **Pump:** On "DINING - SEPTIC" circuit

### Water Heater
- **Model:** Bradford White MI150S6DS21
- **Serial:** ZB2783563
- **Age:** 25 years (Feb 2001)
- **Status:** CRITICAL FAILURE RISK (Expected life 8-12 years)
- **Action:** Replace immediately

subsections.title: Water Heater (Critical)
subsections.content:
### ⚠️ URGENT: Replacement Needed
**Bradford White Water Heater**
- **Model:** MI150S6DS21
- **Serial:** ZB2783563
- **Age:** 25 years (Feb 2001)
- **Status:** CRITICAL FAILURE RISK

**Why This Is Urgent:**
At 25 years old, this unit is more than double its expected lifespan. Risks include catastrophic tank failure, flooding, and safety hazards.

**Immediate Actions:**
1. Check daily for leaks.
2. Listen for unusual noises.
3. **Schedule replacement immediately.**

2. **Budget for immediate replacement.**

sections.title: Main House
sections.content:
**Status:** Partially Documented

### Irrigation System
- **Type:** Sprinkler system
- **Controller:** Rain Bird ESP-ME3
- **Location:** Wall-mounted cabinet
- **Features:** LNK WiFi Ready, Weather Sensor compatible, Seasonal Adjust, Flow Sensor compatible
- **Programs:** A, B, C, D
- **Current Status:** OFF (as of last photo)
- **Coverage:** Landscaping around Main House
- **Map:** Hand-drawn map available showing all head locations

### Laundry Appliances

![Main House - Washer and Dryer - Side Entry](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/BpqXVwoEBvltVIwQ.jpg)

**Washer:**
- **Brand:** Maytag
- **Model:** MFWS700SQ1
- **Serial:** CSU3707671
- **Type:** Front-load washer
- **Location:** Side entry laundry room
- **Age:** ~15 years (est. 2011)
- **Recommended Replacement:** 2026 (typical lifespan 10-15 years)

![Washer Model Label](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/VOkRXpzaMJQNYOFW.jpg)

**Dryer:**
- **Brand:** Maytag
- **Model:** MED9700SQ0
- **Serial:** 65R13978
- **Type:** Electric dryer (240V/60Hz/24A)
- **Type Code:** DDT7-ELE-240/60/24-EL54
- **Location:** Side entry laundry room
- **Age:** ~15 years (est. 2011)
- **Recommended Replacement:** 2026 (typical lifespan 10-15 years)

![Dryer Model Label](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/KYBRNYSBFeEhKfFt.jpg)

**Maintenance Notes:**
- Both units are approaching end of expected lifespan
- Consider replacement as a matching set
- Ensure proper venting and lint trap cleaning for dryer

### Electrical Systems
- **Entry Utility Room Panel:** [Photo Pending]
  *Contains Generator Transfer Switch and Fire Extinguisher.*
- **Basement Panel:** [Photo Pending]
  *QO Load Center with breakers for Heating, Water Heater, and Basement circuits.*
- **Main House Disconnect:** [Photo Pending]
  *Located in Camp Kitchen Utility Room.*
- **Service Type:** [Overhead/Underground]
- **Amperage:** [Amps]
- **Generator:** [Yes/No - Details if yes]
- **Solar Panels:** [Yes/No - Details if yes]

### Water Shutoff
- **Main Shutoff Valve:** [Photo Pending]
  *Location and instructions to be documented.*
- **Exterior Spigots:** [Photo Pending]
  *Locations to be documented.*

subsections.title: Appliances
subsections.content:
### Laundry Appliances

![Main House - Washer and Dryer - Side Entry](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/BpqXVwoEBvltVIwQ.jpg)

**Washer:**
- **Brand:** Maytag
- **Model:** MFWS700SQ1
- **Serial:** CSU3707671
- **Type:** Front-load washer
- **Location:** Side entry laundry room
- **Age:** ~15 years (est. 2011)
- **Recommended Replacement:** 2026 (typical lifespan 10-15 years)

![Washer Model Label](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/VOkRXpzaMJQNYOFW.jpg)

**Dryer:**
- **Brand:** Maytag
- **Model:** MED9700SQ0
- **Serial:** 65R13978
- **Type:** Electric dryer (240V/60Hz/24A)
- **Type Code:** DDT7-ELE-240/60/24-EL54
- **Location:** Side entry laundry room
- **Age:** ~15 years (est. 2011)
- **Recommended Replacement:** 2026 (typical lifespan 10-15 years)

![Dryer Model Label](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/KYBRNYSBFeEhKfFt.jpg)

**Maintenance Notes:**
- Both units are approaching end of expected lifespan
- Consider replacement as a matching set
- Ensure proper venting and lint trap cleaning for dryer
